name: EA-TOGAF Ontology (TOGAF ADM Methodology)
description: TOGAF Architecture Development Method ontology covering ADM phases (Preliminary through Phase H and Requirements Management), architecture artifacts (catalogs, matrices, diagrams, deliverables), building blocks, requirements traceability, stakeholder management, and transition architectures.

components.ADMPhase: Phase in the TOGAF Architecture Development Method cycle
components.ArchitectureDomain: One of the four TOGAF architecture domains
components.ArchitectureArtifact: Work product from architecture development activities
components.Catalog: List of building blocks of a specific type
components.Matrix: Grid showing relationships between building blocks
components.Diagram: Visual representation of building blocks and relationships
components.Requirement: Statement of need the architecture must address
components.Stakeholder: Person or team with interests in the architecture
components.TransitionArchitecture: Intermediate architecture state between baseline and target
components.WorkPackage: Set of activities for architecture implementation

versionHistory.changes: Initial TOGAF ADM ontology consolidated from V3 togaf-adm-extension source. 12 entities, 12 relationships, 8 business rules, 3 enumerations. OAA v6.1.0 compliant.

notes: EA-TOGAF provides the TOGAF ADM methodology layer for enterprise architecture. Imports EA-CORE-ONT for base entity references. Covers the full ADM lifecycle from Preliminary through Phase H including artifact management, building block architecture, and implementation governance.
